name: PDE5 inhibitors are not pharmacokinetically interchangeable for tadalafil claims
statement: Tadalafil has materially longer exposure than the shorter-acting ED PDE5 inhibitors: CIALIS labeling reports a 17.5-hour mean terminal half-life, while VIAGRA reports sildenafil and active metabolite half-lives of about 4 hours, vardenafil labeling reports about 4 to 5 hours, and STENDRA reports avanafil about 5 hours. Therefore, acute sildenafil, vardenafil, or avanafil findings should not be treated as equivalent to tadalafil daily or long-duration exposure.
population_or_context: Comparative pharmacokinetic evidence used for agent-directness classification.
quantitative_note: Tadalafil 17.5-hour mean terminal half-life; sildenafil about 4 hours; vardenafil about 4 to 5 hours; avanafil about 5 hours. Seftel 2004 also contrasts shorter sildenafil/vardenafil duration with tadalafil responsiveness up to 36 hours.

source_locator.section: Seftel abstract and DailyMed clinical pharmacology sections 12.3
source_locator.quote_or_paraphrase_note: E-utilities abstract and labels were used to extract duration, half-life, and food-effect differences.

caveats: Duration of responsiveness is not the same as plasma half-life.
CYP3A4 inhibitors can change exposure and washout timing.
Daily tadalafil exposure differs from as-needed tadalafil and from as-needed non-tadalafil PDE5 inhibitors.
population_profile: Label pharmacokinetic data include healthy volunteers and ED/BPH prescribing contexts; direct for exposure comparison, indirect for off-label endpoint outcomes.
tadalafil_exposure: Tadalafil label exposure includes 2.5 to 20 mg and daily/as-needed contexts; adjacent agents are sildenafil 25 to 100 mg, vardenafil 2.5 to 20 mg, and avanafil 50 to 200 mg as-needed label contexts.
class_transfer_limits: Transfer from shorter-acting agents to tadalafil is weak for outcomes sensitive to exposure duration, delayed adverse effects, daily dosing, accumulation, nitrate timing, or continuous NO/cGMP modulation.
indication_context: Regulatory pharmacokinetic and comparative review evidence, not an off-label clinical outcome study.
comparator_context: Cross-agent pharmacokinetic comparison rather than randomized head-to-head clinical comparison.
endpoint_measure: Half-life, duration of responsiveness, food effects, and exposure timing.
effect_estimate: Qualitative and numeric: tadalafil has much longer terminal half-life than sildenafil, vardenafil, or avanafil.
safety_findings: Longer tadalafil exposure supports longer nitrate separation and extra caution with daily tadalafil, CYP3A4 inhibitors, alpha-blockers, antihypertensives, and alcohol.
directness_to_target_population: High for exposure-transfer rules; low for estimating benefit in healthy men age 30 to 50.
confounding_and_bias_limits: Label pharmacokinetics and review summaries do not establish equivalence for off-label endpoints or adverse-event rates.
